commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
Adds Slack `message_sent` hook emission for outbound agent replies, returns Slack stream message IDs, and updates Slack delivery and streaming tests.

PR surface: Source +241, Tests +3. Total +244 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main and the latest release route Slack `deliverReplies` through `sendMessageSlack` without `message_sent`, while Telegram and the shared outbound path emit the hook; I did not run a live Slack workspace in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow mechanical repair remains on the PR branch: preserve session correlation and prevent duplicate native-progress hook emission.

target.title: fix(slack): emit message_sent hook on outbound delivery (mirror Telegram)
target.body:
## What

Adds the missing `message_sent` plugin hook emission to `@openclaw/slack`
outbound deliveries. Mirrors the Telegram adapter pattern
(`extensions/telegram/src/bot/delivery.replies.ts` â†’
`emitTelegramMessageSentHooks`).

Fixes #89942.

## Why

`docs/plugins/hooks.md` documents `message_sent` as observation-only for all
successful outbound deliveries. Telegram fires it; Slack silently doesn't.
Plugins observing the hook get a complete blind spot for Slack while
Telegram works out of the box.

## Change

Slack has two outbound reply paths and both needed the hook. The fix mirrors
Telegram's `emitTelegramMessageSentHooks` helper and emits on each.

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `extensions/slack/src/message-sent-hook.ts` | **New file.** `emitSlackMessageSentHooks` + internal-hook helper, a structural mirror of Telegram's pattern. Self-gates on `hookRunner.hasHooks("message_sent")`, so it is a no-op when no plugin observes the hook. |
| `extensions/slack/src/monitor/replies.ts` | Non-streaming + streaming-fallback path. Wrap each `sendMessageSlack` (blocks, text, media) in try/catch: emit `message_sent` with the delivered `messageId` on success, emit `success=false` + rethrow on failure. New params thread the session key and group context through. |
| `extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.ts` | Native-streaming happy path. Emit `message_sent` after `stopSlackStream` finalizes the streamed final reply, and `success=false` on a non-fallback stop failure. Not emitted on the `SlackStreamNotDeliveredError` fallback branch (that re-enters `deliverReplies`, which already emits). |
| `extensions/slack/src/streaming.ts` | `stopSlackStream` now returns the finalized message `ts` from `chat.stopStream` (top-level `ts`, falling back to `message.ts`) so the streaming emit can populate `MessageSentEvent.messageId`, matching parity with the non-streaming path and Telegram. |
| `extensions/slack/src/monitor/send.runtime.ts` | Re-export `SlackSendResult` so `replies.ts` can type the captured send result. |
| `extensions/slack/src/monitor/replies.test.ts` | New `message_sent` tests (success with delivered `messageId`, `success=false` on throw, no-emit when no listener, internal-hook on session key, group-context threading), using a real `initializeGlobalHookRunner` registry with a real plugin hook handler; only the underlying Slack transport is stubbed. |
| `extensions/slack/src/streaming.test.ts` | Tests for the new `stopSlackStream` return value (`messageId` from top-level `ts`, fallback to `message.ts`, empty result when neither is present) plus the updated return-type assertions. |
| `extensions/slack/src/outbound-delivery.test.ts` | Removed. This covered an earlier adapter-level approach that was dropped in favor of emitting on the two actual reply paths above. |

Net: `+424 / -180` across 8 files.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** When `@openclaw/slack` delivers an agent reply to Slack, the `message_sent` plugin hook never fires, even though `docs/plugins/hooks.md` documents it as firing for all successful outbound deliveries and the Telegram adapter emits it. A plugin observing the full outbound lifecycle receives `message_received`, `before_dispatch`, `agent_end`, `reply_payload_sending`, and `message_sending`, but never `message_sent`, despite the message landing in Slack. Root-cause analysis and the unpatched reproduction are in issue #89942.

- **Real environment tested:** macOS, Node v25.4.0. Runtime under test is this PR's branch built from source via `node scripts/build-all.mjs qaRuntime`, which bundles `@openclaw/slack` directly from `extensions/slack/src/` (loaded as the stock `slack` plugin at `2026.6.2`), so it runs the exact TypeScript in this PR rather than an inline edit of an installed package. The gateway ran against an isolated state dir (`OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-fork-state`, a copy of `openclaw.json` + agent auth) so the live install was never touched. Channel was a real Slack workspace, real bot in a real channel (`C0APL029CEN`), Socket Mode, real `xoxb`+`xapp` tokens. Plugin `mst-probe` (local) listened on `message_sent` and logged `event.messageId` on receipt.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

  ```
  # Build the PR branch from source (bundles extensions/slack/src as the stock slack plugin)
  node scripts/build-all.mjs qaRuntime

  # Run the built gateway against an isolated state dir (live install untouched)
  OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-fork-state node openclaw.mjs gateway --port 18799

  # Register the mst-probe plugin (listens on message_sent, logs event.messageId).
  # Trigger a real agent reply by @mentioning the bot in #C0APL029CEN.
  # Repeat for both streaming configs:
  #   1) slack streaming { mode: "partial", nativeTransport: true }  -> dispatch.ts finalizer path
  #   2) slack streaming { mode: "off" }                              -> deliverReplies path
  ```

- **Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):** Live `mst-probe` console output captured from the built-from-source gateway, one real @mention -> agent reply per config:

  | Streaming config | Code path exercised | `message_sent` | `messageId` (real Slack `ts`) |
  | --- | --- | --- | --- |
  | `{ mode: "partial", nativeTransport: true }` | `dispatch.ts` -> `stopSlackStream` finalizer | fired, `success=true` | `1780512442.384839` |
  | `{ mode: "off" }` | `monitor/replies.ts` -> `deliverReplies` | fired, `success=true` | `1780512677.343859` |

  The `messageId` values are real `chat.postMessage` / `chat.stopStream` timestamps returned by Slack for the delivered replies, confirming the hook carries the actual delivered message id on both paths (parity with Telegram). On unpatched stock `2026.5.28` the same probe never received `message_sent` (see issue #89942 for the unpatched capture). Supplemental automated coverage (not a substitute for the live proof above): `pnpm test:extension slack` -> 1244 passed; `pnpm test:contracts:channels` -> 34 passed; `pnpm check` -> typecheck + oxlint (0/0) + policy guards all pass.

- **Observed result after fix:** On both the native-streaming path (`dispatch.ts` finalizer after `stopSlackStream`) and the non-streaming path (`deliverReplies`), the registered `message_sent` listener received an event with `to`, `content`, `success=true`, and `messageId=<real Slack ts>` (the `1780512442.384839` and `1780512677.343859` timestamps above). Send failures emit `success=false` with `error` and then rethrow. Suppressed `NO_REPLY` sends correctly skip emission. This mirrors how the Telegram adapter behaves today.

- **What was not tested:** The internal `message:sent` hook is wired conditionally on a caller-provided `sessionKeyForInternalHooks`; the two `deliverReplies` callers pass `route.sessionKey`, but the standalone CLI `message send` path (`slackChannelOutbound.sendText`) is intentionally out of scope here since it does not go through the agent reply paths this PR targets. Happy to follow up.

- **Proof limitations or environment constraints:** Verification used an isolated `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` copy of the live config so the production install was never modified; the launchd-managed live gateway was temporarily stopped to free the Slack Socket Mode connection for the built-from-source gateway, then restored afterward.

## Notes

- AI-assisted (Claude). Real-behavior reproduction and root-cause
  analysis were done with a custom `mst-probe` plugin on a clean
  local install.
- `CHANGELOG.md` not modified, per CONTRIBUTING.md.
- American English in all new code and comments.
- Allow edits by maintainers enabled on the fork branch.
target.author: rishitamrakar

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
